name: Ember
description: Stay on top of every relationship that matters. Ember is a personal networking CRM — add contacts, draft outreach, log interactions, and get nudged before connections go cold.
author: atharvabhave21

skills.description: First-time setup — connects Notion workspace, collects user context, and unlocks the full plugin
Add a new contact to the Contacts database — supports one-liner fast-track and conversational flow
Draft a first-touch outreach message personalised to the contact and the user's networking purpose
Draft a follow-up message using the contact's interaction history and the 3 most recent logged touchpoints
Log an interaction with a contact into the Interactions database — fast capture with next step tracking
Surface the contacts the user should reconnect with — ranked by urgency, grouped by signal type, capped at 10

commands.description: Start Ember or run first-time setup
Re-run the onboarding and setup flow
Draft a first outreach message to a contact
Draft a follow-up message to an existing contact
Log an interaction with a contact
See who you should reconnect with

notion_mcp.reason: Ember reads and writes to Notion Contacts, Interactions, and Settings databases. All data lives in the user's own Notion workspace — Ember never stores anything externally.
notion_mcp.setup_prompt: To use Ember, you'll need to connect your Notion workspace. Make sure the Notion MCP integration is enabled in your Claude settings.

on_session_start.message: Hey — you have {n} contact{s} overdue for follow-up. Type `/ember nudge` to see them.
on_session_start.description: Silently checks for overdue contacts on session start. Only surfaces a single line if something urgent is found. Complete silence if everything is up to date.

notion_template.description: Pre-built Notion template with Contacts DB, Interactions DB, and Settings page — duplicate to get started in under 2 minutes
